meaning: vt.出较低的价钱vi.叫价过低,叫牌过低
examples: The manufacturers appear to have underbid the contracts by a wider margin than usual.
I flatter myself the beasts that perish could not underbid that, as a low form of consciousness.
Just because we underbid our rivals and got a major order, they have accused us of sharp practice.
We see this in the rare-earth sector, where the Chinese underbid competitors around the world.
As workers compete for scarce jobs and firms underbid each other for sales, wages and prices will come under pressure.
